Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 11:32 ET
Massive index put hedging amid single-name call buying signals institutional dispersion play
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. P/C split shows institutions loading index puts (1.80) while aggressively buying single-name calls (0.92) — classic dispersion setup into NFP.
  • 2. AMD seeing extraordinary call accumulation with 40,000 contracts across May/June strikes despite technical death cross signal.
  • 3. Negative $15B SPY gamma creates amplification risk into Friday's NFP with oversold technicals across mega-cap tech.
NARRATIVE — defensive dispersion setup

The most striking signal today is the extreme P/C divergence between index protection (1.80 ratio) and single-name optimism (0.92 ratio) — the widest spread in recent sessions. This structural positioning suggests institutions are hedging broad market exposure while selectively adding equity risk, amplified by the stock-specific regime (0.31 implied correlation) that makes dispersion trades more efficient. The SPY put wall concentration at massive block sizes (263k, 250k, 154k contracts) into Friday's NFP underscores defensive positioning against macro volatility. AMD's extraordinary call flow stands out against this defensive backdrop, with 20,000 contracts each in May $185 and June $180 strikes totaling $1.2B in premium despite the stock's technical death cross. This mirrors Millennium's Q4 reversal from bearish to conviction long positioning, suggesting the institutional flow is extending existing mega-cap tech accumulation even as RSI hits oversold levels. The negative $15B SPY gamma environment means any NFP surprise will amplify these directional bets significantly. Cross-asset context shows vol divergences across 15 names signaling dislocation between options pricing and flow patterns. The backwardation in 8 names including AMD and ARM suggests elevated event risk priced into near-term options, while the TECH BULL/SKEW FEAR signal in XLE reflects energy's outperformance amid broad tech weakness.

Layer 1: Sentiment -32Layer 2: GEX -$15BLayer 3: P/C split 1.80/0.92Layer 9: NFP Friday
